March 21,2003
Stephen S. Stark, County Counsel
County of Santa Barbara
105 East Anapamu Street, Suite 21
Santa Barbara, CA 93101
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-045
Dear Mr. Stark:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Supervisor Gail
tvtarylalt l:garding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
'Act').' Your request concerns Supervisor Marshall's involvement in an imminent
decision by the Santa Barbara County Board of Supervisors ("Board') whether to adopt
amendments to its grading ordinancg as part of a comprehensive program for the
protection and regeneration of oak trees within the county.
The facts you provide indicate that the maffer of oak tree preservation and
regeneration has been under consideration in Santa Barbara County since 1998.
Beginning in September of 2001, various aspects of this program have been examined
and voted upon by the Board. As recently as March ll,2}O3,the Board considered the
oak tree progrirm.
The question you present involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions,
some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to
past conduct. In addition, the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a
pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. It is inappropriate or otherwise not
in the public interest, grven the totality of the circumstances, to piovide written advice.
(Regulations 18329(b)(8XA), (C) and (F), copy enclosed.) Theiefore, we decline to
provide you with the written advice you request.
' Goverr*"rt Code sections 81000 - gLOl4. Commission regulations appearat Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: It is inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest, given the totality of the circumstances, to provide written advice. Therefore, we decline to provide you with the written advice you request.
sections.question_synthetic: The request sought advice regarding potential conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act related to Supervisor Marshall's involvement in County Board decisions on oak tree preservation and grading ordinance amendments.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the matter involves analysis of interrelated decisions including past conduct, and identical or similar issues are the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter; no traditional QUESTION section but request details provided in body; CONCLUSION section clearly identified; document references pending investigation as reason for declination

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The request sought advice regarding potential conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act related to Supervisor Marshall's involvement in County Board decisions on oak tree preservation and grading ordinance amendments.

CONCLUSION: It is inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest, given the totality of the circumstances, to provide written advice. Therefore, we decline to provide you with the written advice you request.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to Santa Barbara County Counsel regarding Supervisor Marshall's potential conflict of interest in County Board decisions on oak tree preservation, citing pending enforcement investigation and the mix of past and current conduct involved.
